10	namespace ffpa {
11	namespace prefill {
12	// prefill utils: prefetch/load QKV g2s funcs, rescale/softmax funcs etc.
13	
14	// Compile-time sanity checks for the large-d (D > ~128) FFPA kernel
15	// template. All invariants are enforced via static_assert so a bad config
16	// fails at NVCC time instead of silently producing wrong numerics. See
17	// ``ffpa_stages_split_q_large_d_template`` for the full parameter contract.
18	template <const int kHeadDim, const int kMmaAtomM, const int kMmaAtomN,
19	          const int kMmaAtomK, const int kMmaTileSeqLenQ,
20	          const int kMmaTileSeqLenK, const int kMmaTileSeqLenP,
21	          const int kMmaTileHeadDimV, const int kValTileSeqLenQ,
22	          const int kValTileSeqLenK, const int kValTileSeqLenP,
23	          const int kValTileHeadDimV, const int kMmaAccFloat32QK,
24	          const int kMmaAccFloat32PV, const int kOStorageAccFloat32,
25	          const int kPrefetchQK, const int kPrefetchPV, const int kShareSmemQKV,
26	          const int kPersistQs2r, const int kPersistQg2s, const int kRegPipeKV,
27	          const int kStageQK, const int kStagePV, const int kPadQ,
28	          const int kPadK, const int kPadV>
29	__device__ __forceinline__ void check_large_d_compiling_states() {
30	  // Matmul Layout: Q[Br,d]@K^T[d,Bc] NT, P[Br,Bc]@V[Bc,d] NN.
31	  // NOTE: K[Bc,d] with row major means K^T[d,Bc] in col major.
32	  static_assert(kMmaAtomM == 16 && kMmaAtomN == 8 &&
33	                kMmaAtomK == 16);                                // m16n8k16
34	  static_assert(kMmaTileSeqLenQ <= 8 && kMmaTileSeqLenK == 1);   // Q@K^T
35	  static_assert(kMmaTileSeqLenP <= 8 && kMmaTileHeadDimV == 1);  // P@V
36	  static_assert(kValTileSeqLenQ == 1 && kValTileSeqLenK <= 16);  // Q@K^T
37	  static_assert(kValTileSeqLenP == 1 &&
38	                kValTileHeadDimV ==
39	                    (kHeadDim / (kMmaAtomN * kMmaTileHeadDimV)));  // P@V
40	  static_assert(kMmaAccFloat32QK == 0 || kMmaAccFloat32QK == 1);
41	  static_assert(kMmaAccFloat32PV == 0 || kMmaAccFloat32PV == 1);
42	  static_assert(kOStorageAccFloat32 == 0 || kOStorageAccFloat32 == 1);
43	  // Make sure that Br >= Bc, for shared memory reuse.
44	  static_assert((kMmaAtomM * kMmaTileSeqLenQ * kValTileSeqLenQ) >=
45	                (kMmaAtomN * kMmaTileSeqLenK * kValTileSeqLenK));
46	  static_assert(kPrefetchQK == 0 || kPrefetchQK == 1);
47	  static_assert(kPrefetchPV == 0 || kPrefetchPV == 1);
48	  static_assert(kShareSmemQKV == 0 || kShareSmemQKV == 1);
49	  // Persist load Q s2r for headdim < 512, more registers, but still keep O(1)
50	  // SRAM.
51	  static_assert(kPersistQs2r == 0 || kPersistQs2r == 1);
52	  // Persist load Q g2s for headdim < 512, more SRAM, but still keep register
53	  // usage.
54	  static_assert(kPersistQg2s == 0 || kPersistQg2s == 1);
55	  // kPersistQg2s and kPersistQs2r can not both enabled for large d kernel.
56	  static_assert((kPersistQg2s & kPersistQs2r) == 0);
57	  // kPersistQg2s and kShareSmemQKV can not both enabled for large d kernel..
58	  static_assert((kPersistQg2s & kShareSmemQKV) == 0);
59	  // Registers Ping pong double buffers for ldmatrix s2r & mma computation
60	  // overlapping.
61	  static_assert(kRegPipeKV == 0 || kRegPipeKV == 1);
62	  // May apply different multi stages policy for QK and V.
63	  static_assert(kStageQK < 5 && kStageQK > 0);  // QK (<=4)
64	  static_assert(kStagePV < 5 && kStagePV > 0);  // V  (<=4)
65	  static_assert(kPadQ >= 0 && kPadQ % 8 == 0);  // 0,8,16
66	  static_assert(kPadK >= 0 && kPadK % 8 == 0);  // 0,8,16
67	  static_assert(kPadV >= 0 && kPadV % 8 == 0);  // 0,8,16
68	}
69	
70	// Compile-time sanity checks for the small-d (D <= 256) FFPA kernel
71	// template. Enforces the FA-2-style small-d invariants (e.g. kStageQK ==
72	// kStagePV == 1, Br >= Bc, kRegPipeKV and kPersistVs2r mutually exclusive).
73	// See ``ffpa_stages_split_q_small_d_template`` for the full parameter
74	// contract.
75	template <const int kHeadDim, const int kMmaAtomM, const int kMmaAtomN,
76	          const int kMmaAtomK, const int kMmaTileSeqLenQ,
77	          const int kMmaTileSeqLenK, const int kMmaTileSeqLenP,
78	          const int kMmaTileHeadDimV, const int kValTileSeqLenQ,
79	          const int kValTileSeqLenK, const int kValTileSeqLenP,
80	          const int kValTileHeadDimV, const int kMmaAccFloat32QK,
81	          const int kMmaAccFloat32PV, const int kOStorageAccFloat32,
82	          const int kPrefetchQK, const int kPrefetchPV, const int kShareSmemQKV,
83	          const int kPersistQs2r, const int kPersistVs2r, const int kRegPipeKV,
84	          const int kStageQK, const int kStagePV, const int kPadQ,
85	          const int kPadK, const int kPadV>
86	__device__ __forceinline__ void check_small_d_compiling_states() {
87	  // Matmul Layout: Q[Br,d]@K^T[d,Bc] NT, P[Br,Bc]@V[Bc,d] NN.
88	  // NOTE: K[Bc,d] with row major means K^T[d,Bc] in col major.
89	  static_assert(kMmaAtomM == 16 && kMmaAtomN == 8 &&
90	                kMmaAtomK == 16);                                // m16n8k16
91	  static_assert(kMmaTileSeqLenQ <= 8 && kMmaTileSeqLenK == 1);   // Q@K^T
92	  static_assert(kMmaTileSeqLenP <= 8 && kMmaTileHeadDimV == 1);  // P@V
93	  static_assert(kValTileSeqLenQ == 1 && kValTileSeqLenK <= 16);  // Q@K^T
94	  static_assert(kValTileSeqLenP == 1 &&
95	                kValTileHeadDimV ==
96	                    (kHeadDim / (kMmaAtomN * kMmaTileHeadDimV)));  // P@V
97	  static_assert(kMmaAccFloat32QK == 0 || kMmaAccFloat32QK == 1);
98	  static_assert(kMmaAccFloat32PV == 0 || kMmaAccFloat32PV == 1);
99	  static_assert(kOStorageAccFloat32 == 0 || kOStorageAccFloat32 == 1);
100	  // Make sure that Br >= Bc, for shared memory reuse.
101	  static_assert((kMmaAtomM * kMmaTileSeqLenQ * kValTileSeqLenQ) >=
102	                (kMmaAtomN * kMmaTileSeqLenK * kValTileSeqLenK));
103	  static_assert(kPrefetchQK == 0 || kPrefetchQK == 1);
104	  static_assert(kPrefetchPV == 0 || kPrefetchPV == 1);
105	  static_assert(kShareSmemQKV == 0 || kShareSmemQKV == 1);
106	  // Persist load Q s2r for headdim <= 128, more registers.
107	  static_assert(kPersistQs2r == 0 || kPersistQs2r == 1);
108	  // Persist load V s2r for headdim <= 128, more registers.
109	  static_assert(kPersistVs2r == 0 || kPersistVs2r == 1);
110	  if constexpr (kShareSmemQKV) {
111	    // kPersistQs2r must be enabled is set kShareSmemQKV as 1
112	    static_assert(kPersistQs2r == 1);
113	  }
114	  // Registers Ping pong double buffers for ldmatrix s2r & mma
115	  // computation overlapping.
116	  static_assert(kRegPipeKV == 0 || kRegPipeKV == 1);
117	  // kRegPipeKV and kPersistVs2r can not both enabled.
118	  static_assert((kRegPipeKV & kPersistVs2r) == 0);
119	  // May apply different multi stages policy for QK and V.
120	  static_assert(kStageQK < 5 && kStageQK > 0);  // QK (<=4)
121	  static_assert(kStagePV < 5 && kStagePV > 0);  // V  (<=4)
122	  static_assert(kPadQ >= 0 && kPadQ % 8 == 0);  // 0,8,16
123	  static_assert(kPadK >= 0 && kPadK % 8 == 0);  // 0,8,16
124	  static_assert(kPadV >= 0 && kPadV % 8 == 0);  // 0,8,16
125	}
126	
127	// Async g2s (global -> shared) copy for one BrOrBc x kMmaAtomK tile of
128	// Q / K / V. Uses cp.async.ca.shared.global 16B transactions, with SMEM
129	// destination addressing chosen by ``kPad`` (swizzle when kPad == 0,
130	// padded layout otherwise). Rows whose global index >= ``seqlen_bound``
131	// are zero-filled via cp.async src-size=0 so that tail tiles with
132	// N % Br != 0 or N % Bc != 0 stay correct without extra branching.
133	//
134	// - ``BrOrBc``    tile rows in this call (Br for Q, Bc for K/V).
135	// - ``kTileSize`` byte-stride between two cp.async stages in SMEM.
136	// - ``kHeadDim``  global head-dim; ``d_tile_id >= kHeadDim/kMmaAtomK``
137	//                 early-returns so callers can issue fixed-length loops.
138	// - ``n_tile_id`` seqlen tile index (Q_tile_id for Q, tile_K_seqlen for K/V).
139	// - ``d_tile_id`` head-dim sub-tile index; clamped via the early return.
140	// - ``stage``     cp.async pipeline stage (multi-buffer ring index).
141	template <const int BrOrBc, const int kTileSize, const int kHeadDim,
142	          const int kMmaAtomK, const int kNumThreads, const int kPad,
143	          typename kDataType = __half>
144	__device__ __forceinline__ void cp_async_qkv_g2s(
145	    uint32_t smem_base_ptr,     // QKV smem base ptr
146	    const kDataType* gmem_ptr,  // QKV gmem ptr
147	    const int gmem_offset,      // QKV gmem_offset
148	    const int n_tile_id,  // seqlen offset, Q_tile_id * Br, tile_K_seqlen * Bc
149	    const int d_tile_id,  // headdim offset, tile_K_d * kMmaAtomK, tile_V_d *
150	                          // kMmaAtomN * 2
151	    const int stage,      // stage * QKV tile_size
152	    const int seqlen_bound = INT_MAX  // bound on the seqlen axis; rows with
153	                                      // global idx >= seqlen_bound are
154	                                      // zero-filled via cp.async src-size=0
155	) {
156	  // QK: tile_K_d < (kHeadDim / kMmaAtomK)
157	  //  V: tile_V_d < (kHeadDim / kMmaAtomN * 2)
158	  if (d_tile_id >= (kHeadDim / kMmaAtomK)) {
159	    return;
160	  }
161	  const int tid = threadIdx.x;  // within block
162	  constexpr bool kSwizzle = (kPad == 0) ? true : false;
163	  // Mapping QKV tid -> smem, tile size [64/128, 16]
164	  // Br 64, tid / 2, row 0~64
165	  const int load_smem_BrOrBc = (tid / (kNumThreads / BrOrBc));
166	  // (tid % 2) * 8, 0,8,...
167	  const int load_smem_d =
168	      (tid % (kNumThreads / BrOrBc)) * (kMmaAtomK / (kNumThreads / BrOrBc));
169	  // Mapping QKV tid -> gmem, tile size [64/128, 16], row offset by
170	  // n_tile_id(seqlen), col offset by d_tile_id(Headdim).
171	  const int load_gmem_BrOrBc = (n_tile_id * BrOrBc) + load_smem_BrOrBc;
172	  const int load_gmem_d = (d_tile_id * kMmaAtomK) + load_smem_d;  // 0,8
173	  // Offset by QKV global gmem_offset.
174	  const int load_gmem_addr =
175	      (gmem_offset + load_gmem_BrOrBc * kHeadDim + load_gmem_d);
176	  // Seqlen boundary predicate: rows beyond QKV_seqlen must be zero-filled.
177	  const bool row_valid = (load_gmem_BrOrBc < seqlen_bound);
178	
179	// cp async & apply swizzle or padding.
180	#pragma unroll
181	  for (int i = 0; i < (kMmaAtomK / (kNumThreads / BrOrBc)); i += 8) {
182	    const uint32_t load_smem_ptr =
183	        (smem_base_ptr +
184	         (stage * kTileSize + load_smem_BrOrBc * (kMmaAtomK + kPad) +
185	          (kSwizzle
186	               ? swizzle::permuted<kMmaAtomK>(load_smem_BrOrBc, load_smem_d + i)
187	               : load_smem_d + i)) *
188	             sizeof(kDataType));
189	    cp_async::cp_async_zfill<16>(load_smem_ptr, &(gmem_ptr[load_gmem_addr + i]),
190	                                 row_valid);
191	  }
192	  // cp_async::commit_group();
193	}
194	
195	// Sync s2r (shared -> register) load of one MMA fragment of Q, K, or V
196	// using ``ldmatrix.sync``. Selects ``ldmatrix.m8n8.x2``, ``x2.trans``
197	// (for V, emits col-major fragments consumed by P@V), or ``x4`` (for Q)
198	// based on ``kTrans`` / ``kNumRegs``. SMEM addressing mirrors the g2s
199	// layout (swizzle when kPad == 0, else padded).
200	//
201	// ``kSmemColStride`` (optional, default 0 = "natural" per-branch stride):
202	// overrides the row stride and the swizzle width used by the K/V
203	// branches. Set to a value > kMmaAtomK (16, 32, 64) to read from a
204	// wider "super-tile" smem layout where one TMA box holds
205	// ``kSmemColStride / kMmaAtomK`` consumed sub-tiles in a single wider
206	// row. ``subtile_col_offset`` selects which sub-tile within that row
207	// the current ldmatrix should target (0 / 16 / 32 / 48 fp16 cols).
208	// The Q branch (kNumRegs == 4) ignores both knobs.
209	template <const int kTrans, const int kNumRegs, const int kTileSize,
210	          const int kMmaAtomM, const int kMmaAtomN, const int kMmaAtomK,
211	          const int kPad, typename kDataType = __half,
212	          const int kSmemColStride = 0>
213	__device__ __forceinline__ void sync_fetch_qkv_frags_s2r(
214	    uint32_t smem_base_ptr,  // QKV smem base ptr
215	    uint32_t* R,             // Register ptr, R_QKV
216	    const int mma_tile_id,   // Q warp_QP 0~num MMAs, KV warp_KV 0
217	    const int warp_tile_id,  // Q 0, KV 0~kValTileSeqLenK
218	    const int n_tile_id,     // seqlen QK 0, V tile_V_Bc
219	    const int stage,         // ring index into smem
220	    const int subtile_col_offset =
221	        0)  // sub-tile col base inside a wide TMA box
222	{
223	  const int lane_id = threadIdx.x % WARP_SIZE;  // 0~31
224	  constexpr bool kSwizzle = (kPad == 0) ? true : false;
225	  if constexpr (kTrans) {
226	    // load V m8n8x2 via ldmatrix.x2.trans
227	    static_assert(kNumRegs == 2);
228	    // mma_tile_id = warp_KV == 0, warp_tile_id = (j % 2), n_tile_id = tile_V_Bc
229	    // warp_smem_V_d  = warp_KV * (kMmaAtomN * kValTileHeadDimV) + (j % 2) *
230	    // kMmaAtomN;
231	    constexpr int kVRowStride =
232	        (kSmemColStride > 0) ? kSmemColStride : (kMmaAtomN * 2);
233	    constexpr int kVSwizzleW =
234	        (kSmemColStride > 0) ? kSmemColStride : (kMmaAtomN * 2);
235	    const int warp_smem_d = warp_tile_id * kMmaAtomN;
236	    const int lane_smem_Bc = n_tile_id * kMmaAtomK + lane_id % 16;
237	    const int lane_smem_d =
238	        subtile_col_offset + warp_smem_d;  // 0,8 (+ box subtile base)
239	    const uint32_t lane_smem_ptr =
240	        (smem_base_ptr +
241	         (stage * kTileSize + lane_smem_Bc * (kVRowStride + kPad) +
242	          (kSwizzle ? swizzle::permuted<kVSwizzleW>(lane_smem_Bc, lane_smem_d)
243	                    : lane_smem_d)) *
244	             sizeof(kDataType));
245	    mma::ldmatrix_m8n8x2_trans(&R[0], &R[1], lane_smem_ptr);
246	  } else {
247	    static_assert(kNumRegs == 2 || kNumRegs == 4);
248	    if constexpr (kNumRegs == 4) {
249	      // load Q m8n8x4 via ldmatrix.x4
250	      // mma_tile_id = warp_QP, kValTileSeqLenQ=1
251	      // warp_smem_Q_Br = warp_QP * (kMmaAtomM * kValTileSeqLenQ) + 0 *
252	      // kMmaAtomM
253	      const int warp_smem_Br = mma_tile_id * (kMmaAtomM);
254	      const int lane_smem_Br = warp_smem_Br + lane_id % 16;  // 0~15
255	      const int lane_smem_d = (lane_id / 16) * 8;            // 0,8
256	      const uint32_t lane_smem_ptr =
257	          (smem_base_ptr +
258	           (stage * kTileSize + lane_smem_Br * (kMmaAtomK + kPad) +
259	            (kSwizzle ? swizzle::permuted<kMmaAtomK>(lane_smem_Br, lane_smem_d)
260	                      : lane_smem_d)) *
261	               sizeof(kDataType));
262	      mma::ldmatrix_m8n8x4(&R[0], &R[1], &R[2], &R[3], lane_smem_ptr);
263	    } else {
264	      // load K m8n8x2 via ldmatrix.x2
265	      // mma_tile_id = warp_KV == 0, warp_tile_id = j
266	      // warp_smem_Bc = warp_KV * (kMmaAtomN * kValTileSeqLenK) + j * kMmaAtomN;
267	      constexpr int kKRowStride =
268	          (kSmemColStride > 0) ? kSmemColStride : kMmaAtomK;
269	      constexpr int kKSwizzleW =
270	          (kSmemColStride > 0) ? kSmemColStride : kMmaAtomK;
271	      const int warp_smem_Bc = warp_tile_id * kMmaAtomN;
272	      const int lane_smem_Bc = warp_smem_Bc + lane_id % 8;  // 0~7
273	      const int lane_smem_d =
274	          subtile_col_offset + ((lane_id / 8) % 2) * 8;  // 0,8 (+ box subtile)
275	      const uint32_t lane_smem_ptr =
276	          (smem_base_ptr +
277	           (stage * kTileSize + lane_smem_Bc * (kKRowStride + kPad) +
278	            (kSwizzle ? swizzle::permuted<kKSwizzleW>(lane_smem_Bc, lane_smem_d)
279	                      : lane_smem_d)) *
280	               sizeof(kDataType));
281	      mma::ldmatrix_m8n8x2(&R[0], &R[1], lane_smem_ptr);
282	    }
283	  }
284	}
285	
286	// Apply a -inf mask to R_S fragments whose local KV column falls beyond the
287	// valid KV length of the current tile. Used on the last KV tile when
288	// ``QKV_seqlen % Bc != 0`` so that online safe-softmax treats padding
289	// columns as logits of -inf (exp(-inf)=0), making them invisible to both
290	// row_max and row_sum without changing the main-path performance.
291	//
292	// The fragment-to-column mapping follows the ``m16n8k16`` C-layout:
293	// for j in [0, kValTileSeqLenK), R_S[j] covers local columns
294	// ``j*8 + (lane_id%4)*2 + {0, 1}`` (and two rows offset by 0 / 8).
295	// With ``kMmaTileSeqLenK == 1`` the warp_KV base is always 0.
296	template <const int kValTileSeqLenK, const int kMmaAccFloat32,
297	          typename kDataType = __half>
298	__device__ __forceinline__ void sync_apply_kv_mask(
299	    uint32_t* R_S,  // &R_S[0][0][0]
300	    const int
301	        kv_valid_local)  // valid KV columns inside this Bc-tile, in (0, Bc]
302	{
303	  using Traits = DtypeTraits<kDataType>;
304	  const int lane_id = threadIdx.x % WARP_SIZE;
305	  const int col_base = (lane_id % 4) * 2;  // local col within 8-col fragment
306	  if constexpr (kMmaAccFloat32) {
307	#pragma unroll
308	    for (int j = 0; j < kValTileSeqLenK; ++j) {
309	      float* t_fptr = reinterpret_cast<float*>(R_S + j * 4);
310	      const int c0 = j * 8 + col_base;
311	      const int c1 = c0 + 1;
312	      if (c0 >= kv_valid_local) {
313	        t_fptr[0] = -INFINITY;
314	        t_fptr[2] = -INFINITY;
315	      }
316	      if (c1 >= kv_valid_local) {
317	        t_fptr[1] = -INFINITY;
318	        t_fptr[3] = -INFINITY;
319	      }
320	    }
321	  } else {
322	    static_assert(
323	        std::is_same_v<kDataType, __half>,
324	        "MMA Acc F16 mask path is only valid for __half activation dtype.");
325	    // fp16 acc: 4 half values packed into 2 uint32; -inf in fp16 = 0xFC00.
326	    const kDataType neg_inf = Traits::from_float(-INFINITY);
327	#pragma unroll
328	    for (int j = 0; j < kValTileSeqLenK; ++j) {
329	      kDataType* t_hptr = reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(R_S + j * 2);
330	      const int c0 = j * 8 + col_base;
331	      const int c1 = c0 + 1;
332	      if (c0 >= kv_valid_local) {
333	        t_hptr[0] = neg_inf;
334	        t_hptr[2] = neg_inf;
335	      }
336	      if (c1 >= kv_valid_local) {
337	        t_hptr[1] = neg_inf;
338	        t_hptr[3] = neg_inf;
339	      }
340	    }
341	  }
342	}
343	
344	// Apply a causal-attention -inf mask to R_S fragments whose local KV
345	// column falls strictly above the allowed causal boundary for the
346	// fragment's global Q row. Called only on tiles that straddle or lie
347	// beyond the causal diagonal (selected by the launcher) so no overhead
348	// is paid on non-causal paths.
349	//
350	// Convention: queries are aligned to the *tail* of the KV sequence so
351	// that global q position in KV space is ``q_pos = row + kv_offset``
352	// with ``kv_offset = Nkv - Nq >= 0``. A key is visible iff
353	// ``k_pos <= q_pos``. This matches the standard decoding-style causal
354	// mask and degenerates to the usual triangular mask when ``Nkv == Nq``.
355	//
356	// The fragment-to-(row,col) mapping follows the ``m16n8k16`` C-layout
357	// (same mapping used by ``sync_apply_kv_mask``):
358	//   row0 = warp_QP*16 + lane_id/4,   row8 = row0 + 8
359	//   col_base = (lane_id % 4) * 2,    per-j cols in [j*8+col_base,
360	//   j*8+col_base+1]
361	// Each thread owns four values per fragment j: {row0,c0}, {row0,c1},
362	// {row8,c0}, {row8,c1}; they may share a single row threshold when the
363	// tile is fully below the diagonal for one sub-row but not the other.
364	template <const int kValTileSeqLenK, const int kMmaAccFloat32,
365	          typename kDataType = __half>
366	__device__ __forceinline__ void sync_apply_causal_mask(
367	    uint32_t* R_S,        // &R_S[0][0][0]
368	    const int warp_QP,    // warp row index (matches kernel state)
369	    const int Br_base,    // Q_tile_id * Br (global Q row of tile's row 0)
370	    const int Bc_base,    // tile_K_seqlen * Bc (global KV col of tile's col 0)
371	    const int kv_offset)  // Nkv - Nq (>= 0 enforced at launch time)
372	{
373	  using Traits = DtypeTraits<kDataType>;
374	  const int lane_id = threadIdx.x % WARP_SIZE;
375	  const int row_base = warp_QP * 16 + (lane_id / 4);
376	  const int col_base = (lane_id % 4) * 2;
377	  const int thresh_row0 = (Br_base + row_base) + kv_offset;
378	  const int thresh_row8 = thresh_row0 + 8;
379	  if constexpr (kMmaAccFloat32) {
380	#pragma unroll
381	    for (int j = 0; j < kValTileSeqLenK; ++j) {
382	      float* t_fptr = reinterpret_cast<float*>(R_S + j * 4);
383	      const int k0 = Bc_base + j * 8 + col_base;
384	      const int k1 = k0 + 1;
385	      if (k0 > thresh_row0)
386	        t_fptr[0] = -INFINITY;
387	      if (k1 > thresh_row0)
388	        t_fptr[1] = -INFINITY;
389	      if (k0 > thresh_row8)
390	        t_fptr[2] = -INFINITY;
391	      if (k1 > thresh_row8)
392	        t_fptr[3] = -INFINITY;
393	    }
394	  } else {
395	    static_assert(std::is_same_v<kDataType, __half>,
396	                  "MMA Acc F16 causal mask path is only valid for __half "
397	                  "activation dtype.");
398	    const kDataType neg_inf = Traits::from_float(-INFINITY);
399	#pragma unroll
400	    for (int j = 0; j < kValTileSeqLenK; ++j) {
401	      kDataType* t_hptr = reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(R_S + j * 2);
402	      const int k0 = Bc_base + j * 8 + col_base;
403	      const int k1 = k0 + 1;
404	      if (k0 > thresh_row0)
405	        t_hptr[0] = neg_inf;
406	      if (k1 > thresh_row0)
407	        t_hptr[1] = neg_inf;
408	      if (k0 > thresh_row8)
409	        t_hptr[2] = neg_inf;
410	      if (k1 > thresh_row8)
411	        t_hptr[3] = neg_inf;
412	    }
413	  }
414	}
415	
416	// Scalar load for one additive attention-bias element. This helper stays
417	// scalar on purpose: the generic mask path supports 4-D broadcasting
418	// (including stride_n == 0), arbitrary row strides from expanded masks,
419	// tail KV tiles where only one of {k0, k1} may be valid, and a runtime bias
420	// dtype. A vectorized load is only safe for a narrower fast path with
421	// stride_n == 1, proven row alignment, both columns in bounds, and a
422	// dtype-specialized branch.
423	__device__ __forceinline__ float load_attn_bias_value(const void* attn_bias,
424	                                                      const int dtype,
425	                                                      const long long offset) {
426	  if (dtype == 1) {
427	    return __half2float(reinterpret_cast<const __half*>(attn_bias)[offset]);
428	  }
429	  if (dtype == 2) {
430	    return __bfloat162float(
431	        reinterpret_cast<const __nv_bfloat16*>(attn_bias)[offset]);
432	  }
433	  return reinterpret_cast<const float*>(attn_bias)[offset];
434	}
435	
436	// Reproduce Triton's ``tl.randint4x`` Philox-4x32-10 counter transform.
437	// The CUDA forward dropout path uses this exact round function so a logical
438	// attention score consumes the same random uint32 as the Triton forward kernel
439	// and PyTorch SDPA efficient attention. ``quad_offset`` addresses a group of
440	// four consecutive random elements; the caller selects one lane from the
441	// returned uint4.
442	__device__ __forceinline__ uint4 philox4x32_10(
443	    const unsigned long long seed, const unsigned long long quad_offset) {
444	  constexpr unsigned int kKeyA = 0x9E3779B9U;
445	  constexpr unsigned int kKeyB = 0xBB67AE85U;
446	  constexpr unsigned int kRoundA = 0xD2511F53U;
447	  constexpr unsigned int kRoundB = 0xCD9E8D57U;
448	  uint4 counter =
449	      make_uint4(static_cast<unsigned int>(quad_offset),
450	                 static_cast<unsigned int>(quad_offset >> 32), 0U, 0U);
451	  unsigned int key0 = static_cast<unsigned int>(seed);
452	  unsigned int key1 = static_cast<unsigned int>(seed >> 32);
453	
454	#pragma unroll
455	  for (int round = 0; round < 10; ++round) {
456	    const unsigned int old0 = counter.x;
457	    const unsigned int old2 = counter.z;
458	    counter.x = __umulhi(kRoundB, old2) ^ counter.y ^ key0;
459	    counter.z = __umulhi(kRoundA, old0) ^ counter.w ^ key1;
460	    counter.y = kRoundB * old2;
461	    counter.w = kRoundA * old0;
462	    key0 += kKeyA;
463	    key1 += kKeyB;
464	  }
465	  return counter;
466	}
467	
468	__device__ __forceinline__ unsigned int select_philox_lane(
469	    const uint4 values, const unsigned int lane) {
470	  unsigned int value = values.x;
471	  if (lane == 1U) {
472	    value = values.y;
473	  } else if (lane == 2U) {
474	    value = values.z;
475	  } else if (lane == 3U) {
476	    value = values.w;
477	  }
478	  return value;
479	}
480	
481	__device__ __forceinline__ float uniform_from_philox_uint(
482	    const unsigned int value) {
483	  return (static_cast<float>(value) + 1.0f) * 2.3283064365386963e-10f;
484	}
485	
486	// Convert one SDPA/Triton logical RNG element offset into a uniform value in
487	// ``(0, 1]``. Triton maps ``element_offset`` to ``quad_offset = offset / 4``
488	// plus a lane inside the uint4 returned by ``tl.randint4x``; mirroring that
489	// mapping here keeps CUDA dropout masks bit-aligned with the Triton forward
490	// kernel and SDPA when Python reserves the same Philox seed/offset range.
491	__device__ __forceinline__ float curand_uniform_from_element_offset(
492	    const unsigned long long seed, const unsigned long long element_offset) {
493	  const uint4 values = philox4x32_10(seed, element_offset >> 2);
494	  const unsigned int lane = static_cast<unsigned int>(element_offset & 3ULL);
495	  return uniform_from_philox_uint(select_philox_lane(values, lane));
496	}
497	
498	template <typename kDataType>
499	__device__ __forceinline__ kDataType
500	apply_dropout_uniform(const kDataType p, const float uniform,
501	                      const float dropout_p, const float keep_scale) {
502	  using Traits = DtypeTraits<kDataType>;
503	  const float keep = (uniform > dropout_p) ? keep_scale : 0.0f;
504	  return Traits::from_float(Traits::to_float(p) * keep);
505	}
506	
507	template <typename kDataType>
508	__device__ __forceinline__ void apply_dropout_pair(
509	    kDataType& p0, kDataType& p1, const bool valid0, const bool valid1,
510	    const unsigned long long element_offset0, const float dropout_p,
511	    const float keep_scale, const unsigned long long philox_seed) {
512	  if (!(valid0 || valid1)) {
513	    return;
514	  }
515	
516	  const unsigned long long element_offset1 = element_offset0 + 1ULL;
517	  const unsigned long long quad_offset0 = element_offset0 >> 2;
518	  if (valid0 && valid1 && quad_offset0 == (element_offset1 >> 2)) {
519	    const uint4 values = philox4x32_10(philox_seed, quad_offset0);
520	    const unsigned int lane0 =
521	        static_cast<unsigned int>(element_offset0 & 3ULL);
522	    p0 = apply_dropout_uniform(
523	        p0, uniform_from_philox_uint(select_philox_lane(values, lane0)),
524	        dropout_p, keep_scale);
525	    p1 = apply_dropout_uniform(
526	        p1, uniform_from_philox_uint(select_philox_lane(values, lane0 + 1U)),
527	        dropout_p, keep_scale);
528	    return;
529	  }
530	
531	  if (valid0) {
532	    p0 = apply_dropout_uniform(
533	        p0, curand_uniform_from_element_offset(philox_seed, element_offset0),
534	        dropout_p, keep_scale);
535	  }
536	  if (valid1) {
537	    p1 = apply_dropout_uniform(
538	        p1, curand_uniform_from_element_offset(philox_seed, element_offset1),
539	        dropout_p, keep_scale);
540	  }
541	}
542	
543	// Apply attention dropout to the already-softmaxed P fragments before P@V.
544	// RNG indexing follows the logical score tensor layout [B, Hq, Nq, Nkv]:
545	// ``linear = ((b * Hq + h) * Nq + q) * Nkv + k``. This is the same element
546	// order used by the Triton forward dropout kernel and by SDPA's fused kernels,
547	// so with the seed/offset reserved in Python the CUDA path produces the same
548	// dropout mask. Row max and LSE are intentionally left untouched because SDPA
549	// applies dropout after softmax normalization.
550	template <const int kValTileSeqLenK, const int kMmaAccFloat32,
551	          typename kDataType = __half>
552	__device__ __forceinline__ void sync_apply_dropout_to_p(
553	    uint32_t* R_S, const float dropout_p, const unsigned long long philox_seed,
554	    const unsigned long long philox_offset, const int Nb_id, const int Nh_id,
555	    const int Nh, const int warp_QP, const int Br_base, const int Bc_base,
556	    const int Nq, const int Nkv) {
557	  const int lane_id = threadIdx.x % WARP_SIZE;
558	  const int row_base = warp_QP * 16 + (lane_id / 4);
559	  const int col_base = (lane_id % 4) * 2;
560	  const int q0 = Br_base + row_base;
561	  const int q8 = q0 + 8;
562	  const float keep_scale = 1.0f / (1.0f - dropout_p);
563	  const unsigned long long base = (static_cast<unsigned long long>(Nb_id) *
564	                                       static_cast<unsigned long long>(Nh) +
565	                                   static_cast<unsigned long long>(Nh_id)) *
566	                                  static_cast<unsigned long long>(Nq) *
567	                                  static_cast<unsigned long long>(Nkv);
568	
569	#pragma unroll
570	  for (int j = 0; j < kValTileSeqLenK; ++j) {
571	    kDataType* t_hptr =
572	        reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(R_S + j * (kMmaAccFloat32 ? 4 : 2));
573	    const int k0 = Bc_base + j * 8 + col_base;
574	    const int k1 = k0 + 1;
575	    const unsigned long long row0_base =
576	        base + static_cast<unsigned long long>(q0) *
577	                   static_cast<unsigned long long>(Nkv);
578	    const unsigned long long row8_base =
579	        base + static_cast<unsigned long long>(q8) *
580	                   static_cast<unsigned long long>(Nkv);
581	    apply_dropout_pair(
582	        t_hptr[0], t_hptr[1], q0 < Nq && k0 < Nkv, q0 < Nq && k1 < Nkv,
583	        philox_offset + row0_base + static_cast<unsigned long long>(k0),
584	        dropout_p, keep_scale, philox_seed);
585	    apply_dropout_pair(
586	        t_hptr[2], t_hptr[3], q8 < Nq && k0 < Nkv, q8 < Nq && k1 < Nkv,
587	        philox_offset + row8_base + static_cast<unsigned long long>(k0),
588	        dropout_p, keep_scale, philox_seed);
589	  }
590	}
591	
592	// Apply normalized additive attention bias to the raw QK accumulator
593	// fragments before online softmax. Each lane owns two Q rows (q0/q8) and
594	// two adjacent KV columns (k0/k1) per kValTileSeqLenK fragment, matching the
595	// m16n8k16 C-fragment layout used by the QK MMA. The host passes broadcast
596	// strides for [B, H, Nq, Nkv], so a zero stride reuses the same bias value
597	// across that dimension. The softmax helper multiplies QK by ``scale``
598	// internally, while SDPA/Triton define additive bias after scaling; therefore
599	// this routine adds ``bias / scale`` to the raw accumulator via ``inv_scale``.
600	template <const int kValTileSeqLenK, const int kMmaAccFloat32,
601	          typename kDataType = __half>
602	__device__ __forceinline__ void sync_apply_attn_bias(
603	    uint32_t* R_S, const void* attn_bias, const int attn_bias_dtype,
604	    const long long attn_bias_stride_b, const long long attn_bias_stride_h,
605	    const long long attn_bias_stride_m, const long long attn_bias_stride_n,
606	    const int Nb_id, const int Nh_id, const int warp_QP, const int Br_base,
607	    const int Bc_base, const int Nq, const int Nkv, const float inv_scale) {
608	  using Traits = DtypeTraits<kDataType>;
609	  const int lane_id = threadIdx.x % WARP_SIZE;
610	  const int row_base = warp_QP * 16 + (lane_id / 4);
611	  const int col_base = (lane_id % 4) * 2;
612	  const int q0 = Br_base + row_base;
613	  const int q8 = q0 + 8;
614	  const long long base = static_cast<long long>(Nb_id) * attn_bias_stride_b +
615	                         static_cast<long long>(Nh_id) * attn_bias_stride_h;
616	
617	  if constexpr (kMmaAccFloat32) {
618	#pragma unroll
619	    for (int j = 0; j < kValTileSeqLenK; ++j) {
620	      float* t_fptr = reinterpret_cast<float*>(R_S + j * 4);
621	      const int k0 = Bc_base + j * 8 + col_base;
622	      const int k1 = k0 + 1;
623	      if (q0 < Nq && k0 < Nkv) {
624	        const long long offset =
625	            base + static_cast<long long>(q0) * attn_bias_stride_m +
626	            static_cast<long long>(k0) * attn_bias_stride_n;
627	        t_fptr[0] += load_attn_bias_value(attn_bias, attn_bias_dtype, offset) *
628	                     inv_scale;
629	      }
630	      if (q0 < Nq && k1 < Nkv) {
631	        const long long offset =
632	            base + static_cast<long long>(q0) * attn_bias_stride_m +
633	            static_cast<long long>(k1) * attn_bias_stride_n;
634	        t_fptr[1] += load_attn_bias_value(attn_bias, attn_bias_dtype, offset) *
635	                     inv_scale;
636	      }
637	      if (q8 < Nq && k0 < Nkv) {
638	        const long long offset =
639	            base + static_cast<long long>(q8) * attn_bias_stride_m +
640	            static_cast<long long>(k0) * attn_bias_stride_n;
641	        t_fptr[2] += load_attn_bias_value(attn_bias, attn_bias_dtype, offset) *
642	                     inv_scale;
643	      }
644	      if (q8 < Nq && k1 < Nkv) {
645	        const long long offset =
646	            base + static_cast<long long>(q8) * attn_bias_stride_m +
647	            static_cast<long long>(k1) * attn_bias_stride_n;
648	        t_fptr[3] += load_attn_bias_value(attn_bias, attn_bias_dtype, offset) *
649	                     inv_scale;
650	      }
651	    }
652	  } else {
653	    static_assert(std::is_same_v<kDataType, __half>,
654	                  "MMA Acc F16 attention bias path is only valid for __half "
655	                  "activation dtype.");
656	#pragma unroll
657	    for (int j = 0; j < kValTileSeqLenK; ++j) {
658	      kDataType* t_hptr = reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(R_S + j * 2);
659	      const int k0 = Bc_base + j * 8 + col_base;
660	      const int k1 = k0 + 1;
661	      if (q0 < Nq && k0 < Nkv) {
662	        const long long offset =
663	            base + static_cast<long long>(q0) * attn_bias_stride_m +
664	            static_cast<long long>(k0) * attn_bias_stride_n;
665	        const float value =
666	            Traits::to_float(t_hptr[0]) +
667	            load_attn_bias_value(attn_bias, attn_bias_dtype, offset) *
668	                inv_scale;
669	        t_hptr[0] = Traits::from_float(value);
670	      }
671	      if (q0 < Nq && k1 < Nkv) {
672	        const long long offset =
673	            base + static_cast<long long>(q0) * attn_bias_stride_m +
674	            static_cast<long long>(k1) * attn_bias_stride_n;
675	        const float value =
676	            Traits::to_float(t_hptr[1]) +
677	            load_attn_bias_value(attn_bias, attn_bias_dtype, offset) *
678	                inv_scale;
679	        t_hptr[1] = Traits::from_float(value);
680	      }
681	      if (q8 < Nq && k0 < Nkv) {
682	        const long long offset =
683	            base + static_cast<long long>(q8) * attn_bias_stride_m +
684	            static_cast<long long>(k0) * attn_bias_stride_n;
685	        const float value =
686	            Traits::to_float(t_hptr[2]) +
687	            load_attn_bias_value(attn_bias, attn_bias_dtype, offset) *
688	                inv_scale;
689	        t_hptr[2] = Traits::from_float(value);
690	      }
691	      if (q8 < Nq && k1 < Nkv) {
692	        const long long offset =
693	            base + static_cast<long long>(q8) * attn_bias_stride_m +
694	            static_cast<long long>(k1) * attn_bias_stride_n;
695	        const float value =
696	            Traits::to_float(t_hptr[3]) +
697	            load_attn_bias_value(attn_bias, attn_bias_dtype, offset) *
698	                inv_scale;
699	        t_hptr[3] = Traits::from_float(value);
700	      }
701	    }
702	  }
703	}
704	
705	// Online safe-softmax step for one [Br, Bc] S fragment (FA-2 paper algo 1).
706	//   1. warp-reduces a per-row max across the Bc axis.
707	//   2. ``m_new = max(m_old, m_new)`` for numerical stability.
708	//   3. ``P = exp(S * scale - m_new)`` written back into R_S in the
709	//      activation dtype so R_S can be reused as P for the next P@V MMA.
710	//   4. warp-reduces the per-row exp-sum into ``lane_row_sum_new``.
711	// Caller follows up with ``sync_precompute_rescale_factors`` +
712	// ``sync_update_max_expsum`` to fold the new (m, l) into the running
713	// (m_old, l_old). The fp32 accumulator path works for both fp16 and
714	// bf16 activations; the fp16-acc fast path requires kDataType == __half.
715	template <const int kValTileSeqLenK, const int kMmaAccFloat32,
716	          typename kDataType = __half>
717	__device__ __forceinline__ void sync_online_safe_softmax(
718	    uint32_t* R_S,                  // &R_S[0][0][0]
719	    const float scale,              // 1 / sqrt(d)
720	    float* lane_row_max_new,        // &lane_row_max_new[0][0]
721	    float* lane_row_sum_new,        // &lane_row_sum_new[0][0]
722	    float* lane_block_row_max_old,  // &lane_block_row_max_old[0][0]
723	    float* lane_block_row_sum_old   // &lane_block_row_sum_old[0][0]
724	) {
725	  using Traits = DtypeTraits<kDataType>;
726	  if constexpr (kMmaAccFloat32) {
727	    // Row max for [Br,Bc] tile, Thread -> Warp -> Block.
728	    {  // kValTileSeqLenQ = 1
729	// Thread level reduce max across kValTileSeqLenK dim, namely Bc.
730	#pragma unroll
731	      for (int j = 0; j < kValTileSeqLenK; ++j) {
732	        const float* t_fptr_S_0_1 =
733	            reinterpret_cast<float*>(R_S + j * 4);  // &R_S[0][j][0]
734	        // This should be the row max after S = (Q @ K^T) / sqrt(d)
735	        const float tmp_max_0 = max(t_fptr_S_0_1[0], t_fptr_S_0_1[1]) * scale;
736	        const float tmp_max_1 = max(t_fptr_S_0_1[2], t_fptr_S_0_1[3]) * scale;
737	        lane_row_max_new[0] = max(lane_row_max_new[0], tmp_max_0);
738	        lane_row_max_new[1] = max(lane_row_max_new[1], tmp_max_1);
739	      }  // end for kValTileSeqLenK
740	
741	      // Warp level reduce max, warp_size = 4
742	      // Each thread contains the maximum of 2 rows of Br,
743	      // and only the values of T0, T4, ..., T28 are used.
744	      lane_row_max_new[0] = warp::reduce_max<float, 4>(lane_row_max_new[0]);
745	      lane_row_max_new[1] = warp::reduce_max<float, 4>(lane_row_max_new[1]);
746	    }  // end for kValTileSeqLenQ
747	
748	    // static_assert(kValTileSeqLenQ == 1);
749	    // Exp sum and mul scale_factor for [Br,Bc] tile, Thread -> Warp -> Block.
750	    {  // kValTileSeqLenQ = 1
751	      // Use latest global row max without update.
752	      // Br 0, row_id, 0~7,  16~23, 32~39, 48~55;
753	      // Br 1, row_id, 8~15, 24~31, 40~47, 56~63;
754	      // Apply m_new = max(m_old, m_new) here.
755	      const float block_row_max_new_0 =
756	          max(lane_block_row_max_old[0], lane_row_max_new[0]);
757	      const float block_row_max_new_1 =
758	          max(lane_block_row_max_old[1], lane_row_max_new[1]);
759	
760	#pragma unroll
761	      for (int j = 0; j < kValTileSeqLenK; ++j) {
762	        // R_S[][][4] 4 32bit registers with each contains 1 F32 element.
763	        // (x,y) 0~7->{c0, c1}, (z,w)->8~15 {c2, c3}
764	        float* t_fptr_S_0_1 = reinterpret_cast<float*>(R_S + j * 4);
765	        kDataType* t_hptr_S_0_1 = reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(R_S + j * 4);
766	        // P = Exp(S - m_new), fmaf(x, y, z) = x * y + z in registers;
767	        t_fptr_S_0_1[0] =
768	            expf(__fmaf_rn(t_fptr_S_0_1[0], scale, -block_row_max_new_0));
769	        t_fptr_S_0_1[1] =
770	            expf(__fmaf_rn(t_fptr_S_0_1[1], scale, -block_row_max_new_0));
771	        t_fptr_S_0_1[2] =
772	            expf(__fmaf_rn(t_fptr_S_0_1[2], scale, -block_row_max_new_1));
773	        t_fptr_S_0_1[3] =
774	            expf(__fmaf_rn(t_fptr_S_0_1[3], scale, -block_row_max_new_1));
775	        lane_row_sum_new[0] += (t_fptr_S_0_1[0] + t_fptr_S_0_1[1]);
776	        lane_row_sum_new[1] += (t_fptr_S_0_1[2] + t_fptr_S_0_1[3]);
777	        // Update R_S for P[Br,Bc] = Exp(S-m), point wise.
778	        // Also convert F32 -> kDataType for P@V MMA, reuse R_S as P.
779	        t_hptr_S_0_1[0] = Traits::from_float(t_fptr_S_0_1[0]);
780	        t_hptr_S_0_1[1] = Traits::from_float(t_fptr_S_0_1[1]);
781	        t_hptr_S_0_1[2] = Traits::from_float(t_fptr_S_0_1[2]);
782	        t_hptr_S_0_1[3] = Traits::from_float(t_fptr_S_0_1[3]);
783	      }  // end for kValTileSeqLenK
784	
785	      // Warp level reduce sum, warp_size = 4
786	      lane_row_sum_new[0] = warp::reduce_sum<float, 4>(lane_row_sum_new[0]);
787	      lane_row_sum_new[1] = warp::reduce_sum<float, 4>(lane_row_sum_new[1]);
788	    }
789	
790	  } else {
791	    // MMA Acc F16 (only valid when kDataType == __half; bf16 forces
792	    // kMmaAccFloat32==1).
793	    static_assert(
794	        std::is_same_v<kDataType, __half>,
795	        "MMA Acc F16 path is only valid for __half activation dtype.");
796	    // Row max for [Br,Bc] tile, Thread -> Warp -> Block.
797	    {  // kValTileSeqLenQ = 1
798	// Thread level reduce max across kValTileSeqLenK dim, namely Bc.
799	#pragma unroll
800	      for (int j = 0; j < kValTileSeqLenK; ++j) {
801	        const kDataType* t_hptr_S_0_1 =
802	            reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(R_S + j * 2);
803	        // This should be the row max after S = (Q @ K^T) / sqrt(d)
804	        const float tmp_max_0 =
805	            Traits::to_float(Traits::hmax(t_hptr_S_0_1[0], t_hptr_S_0_1[1])) *
806	            scale;
807	        const float tmp_max_1 =
808	            Traits::to_float(Traits::hmax(t_hptr_S_0_1[2], t_hptr_S_0_1[3])) *
809	            scale;
810	        lane_row_max_new[0] = max(lane_row_max_new[0], tmp_max_0);
811	        lane_row_max_new[1] = max(lane_row_max_new[1], tmp_max_1);
812	      }  // end for kValTileSeqLenK
813	
814	      // Warp level reduce max, warp_size = 4
815	      // Each thread contains the maximum of 2 rows of Br,
816	      // and only the values of T0, T4, ..., T28 are used.
817	      lane_row_max_new[0] = warp::reduce_max<float, 4>(lane_row_max_new[0]);
818	      lane_row_max_new[1] = warp::reduce_max<float, 4>(lane_row_max_new[1]);
819	    }  // end for kValTileSeqLenQ
820	
821	    // static_assert(kValTileSeqLenQ == 1);
822	    // Exp sum and mul scale_factor for [Br,Bc] tile, Thread -> Warp -> Block.
823	    {  // kValTileSeqLenQ = 1
824	      // Apply m_new = max(m_old, m_new) here.
825	      const float block_row_max_new_0 =
826	          max(lane_block_row_max_old[0], lane_row_max_new[0]);
827	      const float block_row_max_new_1 =
828	          max(lane_block_row_max_old[1], lane_row_max_new[1]);
829	
830	#pragma unroll
831	      for (int j = 0; j < kValTileSeqLenK; ++j) {
832	        kDataType* t_hptr_S_0_1 = reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(R_S + j * 2);
833	        // P = Exp(S - m_new), fmaf(x, y, z) = x * y + z;
834	        float4 t_reg_S_0_1;
835	        t_reg_S_0_1.x = expf(__fmaf_rn(Traits::to_float(t_hptr_S_0_1[0]), scale,
836	                                       -block_row_max_new_0));
837	        t_reg_S_0_1.y = expf(__fmaf_rn(Traits::to_float(t_hptr_S_0_1[1]), scale,
838	                                       -block_row_max_new_0));
839	        t_reg_S_0_1.z = expf(__fmaf_rn(Traits::to_float(t_hptr_S_0_1[2]), scale,
840	                                       -block_row_max_new_1));
841	        t_reg_S_0_1.w = expf(__fmaf_rn(Traits::to_float(t_hptr_S_0_1[3]), scale,
842	                                       -block_row_max_new_1));
843	        lane_row_sum_new[0] += (t_reg_S_0_1.x + t_reg_S_0_1.y);
844	        lane_row_sum_new[1] += (t_reg_S_0_1.z + t_reg_S_0_1.w);
845	        // Update R_S for P[Br,Bc] = Exp(S-m), point wise.
846	        t_hptr_S_0_1[0] = Traits::from_float(t_reg_S_0_1.x);
847	        t_hptr_S_0_1[1] = Traits::from_float(t_reg_S_0_1.y);
848	        t_hptr_S_0_1[2] = Traits::from_float(t_reg_S_0_1.z);
849	        t_hptr_S_0_1[3] = Traits::from_float(t_reg_S_0_1.w);
850	      }  // end for kValTileSeqLenK
851	
852	      // Warp level reduce sum, warp_size = 4
853	      lane_row_sum_new[0] = warp::reduce_sum<float, 4>(lane_row_sum_new[0]);
854	      lane_row_sum_new[1] = warp::reduce_sum<float, 4>(lane_row_sum_new[1]);
855	    }
856	  }
857	}
858	
859	// Precompute O-rescaling factors ``exp(m_old - m_new)`` for the two
860	// sub-rows owned by this lane. Factored out of the softmax step so the
861	// tiling-O rescaling loop over head-dim can reuse them without repeating
862	// the ``expf`` call. On the first KV tile (``n_tile_id == 0``) m_old
863	// is forced to m_new so the factor is 1 and O starts un-rescaled.
864	__device__ __forceinline__ void sync_precompute_rescale_factors(
865	    float* rescale_o_factor_0,            // rescale factor
866	    float* rescale_o_factor_1,            // rescale factor
867	    const float* lane_row_max_new,        // &lane_row_max_new[0][0]
868	    const float* lane_block_row_max_old,  // &lane_block_row_max_old[0][0]
869	    const int n_tile_id                   // tile_K_seqlen
870	) {
871	  float block_row_max_new_0 = lane_row_max_new[0];
872	  float block_row_max_new_1 = lane_row_max_new[1];
873	  float block_row_max_old_0 = lane_block_row_max_old[0];
874	  float block_row_max_old_1 = lane_block_row_max_old[1];
875	  // NOTE: max(-inf, val) = val.
876	  block_row_max_new_0 = max(block_row_max_old_0, block_row_max_new_0);
877	  block_row_max_new_1 = max(block_row_max_old_1, block_row_max_new_1);
878	  // Avoid inf value while using m_old for rescaling O.
879	  block_row_max_old_0 =
880	      (n_tile_id > 0 ? block_row_max_old_0 : block_row_max_new_0);
881	  block_row_max_old_1 =
882	      (n_tile_id > 0 ? block_row_max_old_1 : block_row_max_new_1);
883	  // Precompute rescale_o_factor_0 & rescale_o_factor_1, avoid redundant exp.
884	  rescale_o_factor_0[0] = expf(block_row_max_old_0 - block_row_max_new_0);
885	  rescale_o_factor_1[0] = expf(block_row_max_old_1 - block_row_max_new_1);
886	}
887	
888	// Apply the rescale ``O_new = exp(m_old - m_new) * O_old + P @ V`` to
889	// one [Br, 8] head-dim slice of the running O accumulator. Called inside
890	// the head-dim loop of P@V so the whole O tensor is updated incrementally
891	// with the latest KV-tile contribution. Supports fp32 and fp16 O storage
892	// independently of the MMA accumulator dtype.
893	template <const int kOStorageAccFloat32, const int kMmaAccFloat32,
894	          typename kDataType = __half>
895	__device__ __forceinline__ void sync_rescaling_tiling_o(
896	    uint32_t* R_D,                    // &R_D[0][0][0]
897	    uint32_t* R_O,                    // &R_O[0]
898	    const float* rescale_o_factor_0,  // rescale factor
899	    const float* rescale_o_factor_1,  // rescale factor
900	    const int n_tile_id,              // tile_K_seqlen
901	    const int d_tile_id               // j
902	) {
903	  using Traits = DtypeTraits<kDataType>;
904	  // Now, we get [Br,8] slice of [Br,d], each warp(MMA) contains m16n8.
905	  // 0. Rescale O: Online rescaling O each tile_K_seqlen step, need m_new,
906	  // m_old. m = max(m_old, m_new), O_new[Br,d] = exp(m_old - m) * O_old + P@V m
907	  // = max(m_old, m_new), l = exp(m_old - m) * l_old + l_new (FA2 paper)
908	  if constexpr (kMmaAccFloat32) {
909	    const float* t_fptr_O_0_1 = reinterpret_cast<float*>(R_O);
910	    if constexpr (kOStorageAccFloat32) {
911	      // (x,y) 0~7->{c0, c1}, (z,w)->8~15 {c2, c3} kValTileSeqLenP=1
912	      float* t_fptr_D_0_1 =
913	          reinterpret_cast<float*>(R_D + d_tile_id * 4);  // &(R_D[0][j][0])
914	      t_fptr_D_0_1[0] =
915	          __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_0[0], t_fptr_D_0_1[0], t_fptr_O_0_1[0]);
916	      t_fptr_D_0_1[1] =
917	          __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_0[0], t_fptr_D_0_1[1], t_fptr_O_0_1[1]);
918	      t_fptr_D_0_1[2] =
919	          __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_1[0], t_fptr_D_0_1[2], t_fptr_O_0_1[2]);
920	      t_fptr_D_0_1[3] =
921	          __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_1[0], t_fptr_D_0_1[3], t_fptr_O_0_1[3]);
922	    } else {
923	      kDataType* t_hptr_D_0_1 =
924	          reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(R_D + d_tile_id * 2);
925	      t_hptr_D_0_1[0] = Traits::from_float(
926	          __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_0[0], Traits::to_float(t_hptr_D_0_1[0]),
927	                    t_fptr_O_0_1[0]));
928	      t_hptr_D_0_1[1] = Traits::from_float(
929	          __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_0[0], Traits::to_float(t_hptr_D_0_1[1]),
930	                    t_fptr_O_0_1[1]));
931	      t_hptr_D_0_1[2] = Traits::from_float(
932	          __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_1[0], Traits::to_float(t_hptr_D_0_1[2]),
933	                    t_fptr_O_0_1[2]));
934	      t_hptr_D_0_1[3] = Traits::from_float(
935	          __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_1[0], Traits::to_float(t_hptr_D_0_1[3]),
936	                    t_fptr_O_0_1[3]));
937	    }
938	  } else {
939	    // MMA Acc F16 (only valid when kDataType == __half; bf16 forces
940	    // kMmaAccFloat32==1).
941	    static_assert(
942	        std::is_same_v<kDataType, __half>,
943	        "MMA Acc F16 path is only valid for __half activation dtype.");
944	    const kDataType* t_hptr_O_0_1 = reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(R_O);
945	    if constexpr (kOStorageAccFloat32) {
946	      // (x,y) 0~7->{c0, c1}, (z,w)->8~15 {c2, c3} kValTileSeqLenP=1
947	      float* t_fptr_D_0_1 = reinterpret_cast<float*>(R_D + d_tile_id * 4);
948	      t_fptr_D_0_1[0] = __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_0[0], t_fptr_D_0_1[0],
949	                                  Traits::to_float(t_hptr_O_0_1[0]));
950	      t_fptr_D_0_1[1] = __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_0[0], t_fptr_D_0_1[1],
951	                                  Traits::to_float(t_hptr_O_0_1[1]));
952	      t_fptr_D_0_1[2] = __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_1[0], t_fptr_D_0_1[2],
953	                                  Traits::to_float(t_hptr_O_0_1[2]));
954	      t_fptr_D_0_1[3] = __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_1[0], t_fptr_D_0_1[3],
955	                                  Traits::to_float(t_hptr_O_0_1[3]));
956	    } else {
957	      kDataType* t_hptr_D_0_1 =
958	          reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(R_D + d_tile_id * 2);
959	      t_hptr_D_0_1[0] = Traits::from_float(
960	          __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_0[0], Traits::to_float(t_hptr_D_0_1[0]),
961	                    Traits::to_float(t_hptr_O_0_1[0])));
962	      t_hptr_D_0_1[1] = Traits::from_float(
963	          __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_0[0], Traits::to_float(t_hptr_D_0_1[1]),
964	                    Traits::to_float(t_hptr_O_0_1[1])));
965	      t_hptr_D_0_1[2] = Traits::from_float(
966	          __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_1[0], Traits::to_float(t_hptr_D_0_1[2]),
967	                    Traits::to_float(t_hptr_O_0_1[2])));
968	      t_hptr_D_0_1[3] = Traits::from_float(
969	          __fmaf_rn(rescale_o_factor_1[0], Traits::to_float(t_hptr_D_0_1[3]),
970	                    Traits::to_float(t_hptr_O_0_1[3])));
971	    }
972	  }
973	}
974	
975	// Fold the new (m_new, l_new) into the running (m_old, l_old) after O has
976	// been rescaled by ``sync_rescaling_tiling_o``. Implements the FA-2
977	// recurrence ``l_new = exp(m_old - m_new) * l_old + rowsum(P)`` and
978	// ``m_old <- max(m_old, m_new)``.
979	__device__ __forceinline__ void sync_update_max_expsum(
980	    float* lane_row_max_new,          // &lane_row_max_new[0][0]
981	    float* lane_row_sum_new,          // &lane_row_sum_new[0][0]
982	    float* lane_block_row_max_old,    // &lane_block_row_max_old[0][0]
983	    float* lane_block_row_sum_old,    // &lane_block_row_sum_old[0][0]
984	    const float* rescale_o_factor_0,  // rescale factor 0 exp(m_old - m_new)
985	    const float* rescale_o_factor_1   // rescale factor 1 exp(m_old - m_new)
986	) {
987	  // Now, we can update m, l after O has been scaled.
988	  // Update l = exp(m_old - m_new) * l_old + row_sum(P).
989	  lane_block_row_sum_old[0] = (__fmaf_rn(
990	      rescale_o_factor_0[0], lane_block_row_sum_old[0], lane_row_sum_new[0]));
991	  lane_block_row_sum_old[1] = (__fmaf_rn(
992	      rescale_o_factor_1[0], lane_block_row_sum_old[1], lane_row_sum_new[1]));
993	  // 2. Then, update block row max for each lane.
994	  lane_block_row_max_old[0] =
995	      max(lane_block_row_max_old[0], lane_row_max_new[0]);
996	  lane_block_row_max_old[1] =
997	      max(lane_block_row_max_old[1], lane_row_max_new[1]);
998	}
999	
1000	// Final O scaling once the KV loop is done: ``O = (1 / l_final) * O`` and
1001	// cast fp32 accumulator back to the activation dtype so the collective
1002	// ``sync_store_o_r2g`` can emit 128-bit global stores.
1003	template <const int kValTileHeadDimV, const int kOStorageAccFloat32,
1004	          typename kDataType = __half>
1005	__device__ __forceinline__ void sync_rescaling_final_o(
1006	    uint32_t* R_D,                       // Final O after loop over N
1007	    const float* lane_block_row_sum_old  // &lane_block_row_sum_old[0][0]
1008	) {
1009	  using Traits = DtypeTraits<kDataType>;
1010	  // Finaly, we still have to rescale O once more.
1011	  // O_output(D) = ( 1/l_final ) * O_final (FA2 paper)
1012	  // static_assert(kValTileSeqLenP == 1);
1013	  {  // kValTileSeqLenP = 1
1014	    const float rescale_factor_0 = __frcp_rn(lane_block_row_sum_old[0]);
1015	    const float rescale_factor_1 = __frcp_rn(lane_block_row_sum_old[1]);
1016	#pragma unroll
1017	    for (int j = 0; j < kValTileHeadDimV; ++j) {  // 8, 16, 32, ...
1018	      // Scaling in registers & convert F32 -> kDataType for O collective store.
1019	      if constexpr (kOStorageAccFloat32) {
1020	        const float* t_fptr_D_0_1 = reinterpret_cast<float*>(R_D + j * 4);
1021	        kDataType* t_hptr_D_0_1 = reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(R_D + j * 4);
1022	        t_hptr_D_0_1[0] =
1023	            Traits::from_float(rescale_factor_0 * t_fptr_D_0_1[0]);
1024	        t_hptr_D_0_1[1] =
1025	            Traits::from_float(rescale_factor_0 * t_fptr_D_0_1[1]);
1026	        t_hptr_D_0_1[2] =
1027	            Traits::from_float(rescale_factor_1 * t_fptr_D_0_1[2]);
1028	        t_hptr_D_0_1[3] =
1029	            Traits::from_float(rescale_factor_1 * t_fptr_D_0_1[3]);
1030	      } else {
1031	        kDataType* t_hptr_D_0_1 = reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(R_D + j * 2);
1032	        t_hptr_D_0_1[0] = Traits::from_float(rescale_factor_0 *
1033	                                             Traits::to_float(t_hptr_D_0_1[0]));
1034	        t_hptr_D_0_1[1] = Traits::from_float(rescale_factor_0 *
1035	                                             Traits::to_float(t_hptr_D_0_1[1]));
1036	        t_hptr_D_0_1[2] = Traits::from_float(rescale_factor_1 *
1037	                                             Traits::to_float(t_hptr_D_0_1[2]));
1038	        t_hptr_D_0_1[3] = Traits::from_float(rescale_factor_1 *
1039	                                             Traits::to_float(t_hptr_D_0_1[3]));
1040	      }
1041	    }  // end for kValTileHeadDimV
1042	  }  // end for kValTileSeqLenP = 1
1043	}
1044	
1045	// Collective write-back of the softmax LSE values from registers to gmem.
1046	// Each 4-thread subgroup within a warp writes the logsumexp for the two Q
1047	// rows it owns: ``lse = log(row_sum) + row_max``.  Thread 0 of each group
1048	// emits both values via plain 32-bit global stores so the write pattern
1049	// matches the O-store row order exactly.
1050	template <const int Br, const int kMmaAtomM, const int kValTileSeqLenQ>
1051	__device__ __forceinline__ void sync_store_lse_r2g(
1052	    float* __restrict__ gmem_lse_ptr, const int gmem_lse_offset,
1053	    const int n_tile_id, const int mma_tile_id,
1054	    const float* lane_block_row_max_old, const float* lane_block_row_sum_old,
1055	    const int seqlen_bound = INT_MAX) {
1056	  // LSE = log(row_sum) + row_max (row_sum = sum_i exp(S_i - row_max))
1057	  const float lse_0 =
1058	      __logf(lane_block_row_sum_old[0]) + lane_block_row_max_old[0];
1059	  const float lse_1 =
1060	      __logf(lane_block_row_sum_old[1]) + lane_block_row_max_old[1];
1061	
1062	  const int lane_id = threadIdx.x % WARP_SIZE;
1063	  // Thread 0 of each 4-thread subgroup writes both rows; row indices mirror
1064	  // sync_store_o_r2g's layout so that LSE[i] corresponds to O[i,:].
1065	  if (lane_id % 4 == 0) {
1066	    const int row_in_warp = lane_id / 4;  // 0..7
1067	    const int global_row_0 =
1068	        n_tile_id * Br + mma_tile_id * kMmaAtomM + row_in_warp;
1069	    const int global_row_1 =
1070	        n_tile_id * Br + mma_tile_id * kMmaAtomM + row_in_warp + 8;
1071	    const int lse_idx_0 = gmem_lse_offset + global_row_0;
1072	    const int lse_idx_1 = gmem_lse_offset + global_row_1;
1073	    if (global_row_0 < seqlen_bound) {
1074	      gmem_lse_ptr[lse_idx_0] = lse_0;
1075	    }
1076	    if (global_row_1 < seqlen_bound) {
1077	      gmem_lse_ptr[lse_idx_1] = lse_1;
1078	    }
1079	  }
1080	}
1081	
1082	// Collective write-back of the final O tile from registers to gmem.
1083	// Uses warp-shuffles across lane_id % 4 to pack the per-thread fragment
1084	// layout into contiguous 128-bit vectors, then ``st.global.v4`` once per
1085	// (lane % 4 == 0) thread. Scratch registers ``R_Q`` / ``R_K`` are reused
1086	// as the shuffle staging buffers so no extra SMEM is needed. Rows with
1087	// global idx >= ``seqlen_bound`` are not written, keeping tail padding
1088	// (N % Br != 0) untouched.
1089	template <const int Br, const int kHeadDim, const int kMmaAtomM,
1090	          const int kMmaAtomN, const int kValTileHeadDimV,
1091	          const int kOStorageAccFloat32, typename kDataType = __half>
1092	__device__ __forceinline__ void sync_store_o_r2g(
1093	    kDataType* gmem_ptr,              // O gmem ptr
1094	    const int gmem_offset,            // O gmem global offset
1095	    const int n_tile_id,              // curr tile id (seqlen) O_tile_id
1096	    const int mma_tile_id,            // Q warp_QP 0~num MMAs, KV warp_KV 0
1097	    uint32_t* R_D,                    // Final scaled O
1098	    uint32_t* R_Q,                    // R_Q[1][4] for registers reuse
1099	    uint32_t* R_K,                    // R_K[8][2] for registers reuse
1100	    const int seqlen_bound = INT_MAX  // per-row bound: rows with global idx >=
1101	                                      // seqlen_bound are not written to gmem
1102	) {
1103	  // Store O(D): Write O[Br,d] from regs -> gmem, collective store
1104	  // with reg reuse & warp shuffle.
1105	  const int lane_id = threadIdx.x % WARP_SIZE;  // 0~31
1106	  // static_assert(kValTileSeqLenP == 1);
1107	  {  // kValTileSeqLenP = 1
1108	#pragma unroll
1109	    for (int j = 0; j < kValTileHeadDimV; ++j) {  // 8
1110	      // reuse R_Q[1][4], R_K[8][2] for collective store.
1111	      uint32_t* t_uptr_Z_0 = reinterpret_cast<uint32_t*>(R_Q);
1112	      uint32_t* t_uptr_Z_1 = reinterpret_cast<uint32_t*>(R_K);
1113	      const int offset = (kOStorageAccFloat32) ? j * 4 : j * 2;
1114	      t_uptr_Z_0[0] = R_D[offset + 0];
1115	      t_uptr_Z_1[0] = R_D[offset + 1];
1116	      t_uptr_Z_0[1] =
1117	          __shfl_sync((0xffffffff), R_D[offset + 0], lane_id + 1, 4);
1118	      t_uptr_Z_0[2] =
1119	          __shfl_sync((0xffffffff), R_D[offset + 0], lane_id + 2, 4);
1120	      t_uptr_Z_0[3] =
1121	          __shfl_sync((0xffffffff), R_D[offset + 0], lane_id + 3, 4);
1122	      t_uptr_Z_1[1] =
1123	          __shfl_sync((0xffffffff), R_D[offset + 1], lane_id + 1, 4);
1124	      t_uptr_Z_1[2] =
1125	          __shfl_sync((0xffffffff), R_D[offset + 1], lane_id + 2, 4);
1126	      t_uptr_Z_1[3] =
1127	          __shfl_sync((0xffffffff), R_D[offset + 1], lane_id + 3, 4);
1128	
1129	      // st.global.v4 128 bits. [Br,d]
1130	      if (lane_id % 4 == 0) {
1131	        // (0/1)*32 + (0/1)*16=(0,16,32,48), + 0~7 -> 0~56 kValTileSeqLenP = 1
1132	        // int store_warp_regs_O_Br = warp_QP * (kMmaAtomM * kValTileSeqLenP ) +
1133	        // 0 * kMmaAtomM;
1134	        const int store_warp_regs_O_Br = mma_tile_id * (kMmaAtomM);
1135	        const int store_lane_gmem_O_Br =
1136	            n_tile_id * Br + store_warp_regs_O_Br + lane_id / 4;  // 0~7
1137	        // (0~3)*16 + (0/1)*8=(0,8,16,24,...,48,56)  warp_KV = 0
1138	        // int store_warp_regs_O_d = warp_KV * (kMmaAtomN * kValTileHeadDimV) +
1139	        // j * kMmaAtomN;
1140	        const int store_warp_regs_O_d = j * kMmaAtomN;
1141	        const int store_lane_gmem_O_d = store_warp_regs_O_d;  // (0~3)*16+(0/8)
1142	        const int store_gmem_O_addr_0 =
1143	            (gmem_offset + (store_lane_gmem_O_Br + 0) * kHeadDim +
1144	             store_lane_gmem_O_d);
1145	        const int store_gmem_O_addr_1 =
1146	            (gmem_offset + (store_lane_gmem_O_Br + 8) * kHeadDim +
1147	             store_lane_gmem_O_d);
1148	        // Row predicate: skip writes past QKV_seqlen to keep output rows
1149	        // beyond the valid length untouched when N % Br != 0.
1150	        if ((store_lane_gmem_O_Br + 0) < seqlen_bound) {
1151	          cp_async::stg_sync_128b(&gmem_ptr[store_gmem_O_addr_0], t_uptr_Z_0);
1152	        }
1153	        if ((store_lane_gmem_O_Br + 8) < seqlen_bound) {
1154	          cp_async::stg_sync_128b(&gmem_ptr[store_gmem_O_addr_1], t_uptr_Z_1);
1155	        }
1156	      }
1157	    }  // end for kValTileHeadDimV
1158	  }  // kValTileSeqLenP = 1
1159	}
1160	
1161	// ---- backward pass helpers ----
1162	
1163	// Reconstruct P from S and LSE: P = exp(S*scale - LSE).
1164	template <const int kValTileSeqLenK, const int kMmaAccFloat32,
1165	          typename kDataType>
1166	__device__ __forceinline__ void sync_compute_p_from_lse(uint32_t* R_S,
1167	                                                        const float* lse_ptr,
1168	                                                        const float scale,
1169	                                                        const int limb) {
1170	  using Traits = DtypeTraits<kDataType>;
1171	  const int lid = threadIdx.x % WARP_SIZE;
1172	  const float l0 = lse_ptr[lid / 4];
1173	  const float l1 = lse_ptr[lid / 4 + 8];
1174	#pragma unroll
1175	  for (int c = 0; c < kValTileSeqLenK; ++c) {
1176	    if constexpr (kMmaAccFloat32) {
1177	      float* fp = reinterpret_cast<float*>(&R_S[c * 4]);
1178	      kDataType* hp = reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(&R_S[c * 2]);
1179	      hp[0] = Traits::from_float(expf(__fmaf_rn(fp[0], scale, -l0)));
1180	      hp[1] = Traits::from_float(expf(__fmaf_rn(fp[1], scale, -l0)));
1181	      hp[2] = Traits::from_float(expf(__fmaf_rn(fp[2], scale, -l1)));
1182	      hp[3] = Traits::from_float(expf(__fmaf_rn(fp[3], scale, -l1)));
1183	    } else {
1184	      kDataType* hp = reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(&R_S[c * 2]);
1185	      float v0 = expf(__fmaf_rn(Traits::to_float(hp[0]), scale, -l0));
1186	      float v1 = expf(__fmaf_rn(Traits::to_float(hp[1]), scale, -l0));
1187	      float v2 = expf(__fmaf_rn(Traits::to_float(hp[2]), scale, -l1));
1188	      float v3 = expf(__fmaf_rn(Traits::to_float(hp[3]), scale, -l1));
1189	      hp[0] = Traits::from_float(v0);
1190	      hp[1] = Traits::from_float(v1);
1191	      hp[2] = Traits::from_float(v2);
1192	      hp[3] = Traits::from_float(v3);
1193	    }
1194	  }
1195	}
1196	
1197	// dS = P * (dP - dP_sum).
1198	template <const int kValTileSeqLenK, const int kMmaAccFloat32,
1199	          typename kDataType>
1200	__device__ __forceinline__ void sync_compute_ds_softmax_gradient(
1201	    uint32_t* R_P, uint32_t* R_dP, const float* dp_sum, const int limb) {
1202	  using Traits = DtypeTraits<kDataType>;
1203	  const int lid = threadIdx.x % WARP_SIZE;
1204	  const float ds0 = dp_sum[lid / 4];
1205	  const float ds1 = dp_sum[lid / 4 + 8];
1206	#pragma unroll
1207	  for (int c = 0; c < kValTileSeqLenK; ++c) {
1208	    float* fd = reinterpret_cast<float*>(&R_dP[c * 4]);
1209	    if constexpr (kMmaAccFloat32) {
1210	      kDataType* hp = reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(&R_P[c * 2]);
1211	      fd[0] = Traits::to_float(hp[0]) * (fd[0] - ds0);
1212	      fd[1] = Traits::to_float(hp[1]) * (fd[1] - ds0);
1213	      fd[2] = Traits::to_float(hp[2]) * (fd[2] - ds1);
1214	      fd[3] = Traits::to_float(hp[3]) * (fd[3] - ds1);
1215	    } else {
1216	      kDataType* hp = reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(&R_P[c * 2]);
1217	      kDataType* hd = reinterpret_cast<kDataType*>(&R_dP[c * 2]);
1218	      fd[0] = Traits::to_float(hp[0]) * (Traits::to_float(hd[0]) - ds0);
1219	      fd[1] = Traits::to_float(hp[1]) * (Traits::to_float(hd[1]) - ds0);
1220	      fd[2] = Traits::to_float(hp[2]) * (Traits::to_float(hd[2]) - ds1);
1221	      fd[3] = Traits::to_float(hp[3]) * (Traits::to_float(hd[3]) - ds1);
1222	    }
1223	  }
1224	}
1225	
1226	}  // namespace prefill
1227	}  // namespace ffpa

# CuTeDSL 后端总览与 SM80/SM90 分发

## 1. 本讲目标

学完本讲，你应当能够：

- 说清 **CuTeDSL 后端**在整个 FFPA 四后端版图里的定位（基于 CUTLASS CuTe DSL，H200 上最快、约束最多）。
- 读懂 `cute/__init__.py` 里三个**能力探测函数**——`cute_forward_available`、`cute_backward_available`、`cute_max_supported_head_dim`——分别负责哪一层门禁。
- 读懂 `_require_cute_supported` 这一**逐调用张量级硬校验**：它在 (arch, head_dim, dtype) 三个维度上各卡了什么、为何只报错不回退。
- 掌握 `_use_sm90_specialized` 这条**单一分发谓词**，并能据此判断任意 `(major, D, D_v)` 组合走「SM90 专用路径」还是「SM80 通用 Split-D 回退路径」。

本讲只讲 CuTeDSL 后端的**入口与分发**，不深入具体 kernel（d384/d512/generic、tile scheduler、pipeline 留给 u6-l3、u6-l4）。承接 u3-4 的「`_FFPAAttnFunc` 前/反向分发」与 u2-l5 的「varlen 逐层校验」结论，本讲只补 CuTeDSL 专属的那一环。

## 2. 前置知识

- **compute capability（SM 架构号）**：NVIDIA GPU 的型号标识，写作 `major.minor`，如 `8.0`（Ampere，A100/A30）、`8.9`（Ada，L20/4090）、`9.0`（Hopper，H100/H200）、`12.0`（Blackwell，RTX PRO 6000）。`major` 是架构大代。本讲里 `major >= 8` 即「Ampere 及以后」。
- **CUTLASS / CuTe DSL**：NVIDIA 开源的高性能矩阵乘库 CUTLASS 提供的 Python「CuTe DSL」，可在运行期用 Python 描述并 JIT 出 GPU kernel，封装了 WGMMA、TMA 等 Hopper 专用指令。CuTeDSL 后端就是用它写出的 FFPA attention kernel。
- **WGMMA / TMA / cp.async**：Hopper 的 warpgroup MMA（WGMMA）与异步张量内存加速器（TMA）是 SM90a 专用指令；Ampere 的 warp 级 MMA 与 `cp.async` 则向前兼容到 SM89/SM90/SM120。这正是 CuTeDSL 要分「SM90 专用」与「SM80 通用」两条路径的硬件根因。
- **回退（fallback）vs 拒绝（raise）**：FFPA 的 Triton 后端在不适合时**静默回退 SDPA**（见 u1-l4、u3-3）；而 CuTeDSL 后端对**不支持的特性**（attn_mask、dropout、未对齐 head_dim 等）一律**抛错**，从不静默降级——这是本讲反复出现的「无静默回退」原则。
- **`[B,H,N,D]` 与 `[B,N,H,D]` 布局**：SDPA 风格头在序列前 `[B,H,N,D]`，FlashAttention / CuTeDSL 原生头在序列后 `[B,N,H,D]`。CuTeDSL 入口在两侧做 transpose 转换（见 u6-l2，本讲只提一句）。

## 3. 本讲源码地图

| 文件 | 本讲用到的部分 | 作用 |
|---|---|---|
| [src/ffpa_attn/cute/\_\_init\_\_.py](https://github.com/xlite-dev/ffpa-attn/blob/882989c2a3aa465867de9ea2dbd19dbed08660d9/src/ffpa_attn/cute/__init__.py) | 全部 4 个最小模块都在这里 | CuTeDSL 后端的入口 shim：能力探测、校验、SM80/SM90 分发、torch custom op 注册 |
| [src/ffpa_attn/cute/\_utils.py](https://github.com/xlite-dev/ffpa-attn/blob/882989c2a3aa465867de9ea2dbd19dbed08660d9/src/ffpa_attn/cute/_utils.py) | 常量与 `dense_min_supported_head_dim` | 提供 `MIN_SUPPORTED_HEAD_DIM=320`、`SM90_SUPPORTED_HEAD_DIM=512`、`SM80_SUPPORTED_HEAD_DIM=1024`、`SM80_FWD_SPLIT_D_CHUNK=32` 等门禁常量 |
| [src/ffpa_attn/cute/README.md](https://github.com/xlite-dev/ffpa-attn/blob/882989c2a3aa465867de9ea2dbd19dbed08660d9/src/ffpa_attn/cute/README.md) | 路由图、硬约束表、Blackwell 调研笔记 | 用文档语言复述本讲的分发规则与已知限制 |
| [src/ffpa_attn/functional.py](https://github.com/xlite-dev/ffpa-attn/blob/882989c2a3aa465867de9ea2dbd19dbed08660d9/src/ffpa_attn/functional.py) | `try/except` 导入、`CuTeDSLBackend`、`fallback()`、前/反向分发 | Python 分发层如何把调用接驳进 CuTeDSL 入口 |

> 提醒：Python **包名**是 `ffpa_attn.cute`，但对外**后端名字符串**是 `"cutedsl"`、配置类是 `CuTeDSLBackend`——三者不字字对应，这是历史命名，记住即可（[src/ffpa_attn/cute/README.md:9-10](https://github.com/xlite-dev/ffpa-attn/blob/882989c2a3aa465867de9ea2dbd19dbed08660d9/src/ffpa_attn/cute/README.md#L9-L10)）。

## 4. 核心概念与源码讲解

### 4.1 CuTeDSL 后端定位与接驳

#### 4.1.1 概念说明

CuTeDSL 是 FFPA 四后端里**性能上限最高、约束也最多**的一个。它用 NVIDIA CUTLASS 的 CuTe DSL 在运行期 JIT 出 GPU kernel，能直接用上 Hopper 的 WGMMA + TMA，因此在 H200 SXM、`D=512` 大 head_dim 场景下可达 **427 TFLOPS**，是当前最快路径（[README.md:87](https://github.com/xlite-dev/ffpa-attn/blob/882989c2a3aa465867de9ea2dbd19dbed08660d9/README.md#L87)）。代价是：

- 只支持 fp16/bf16；
- 不支持 attn_mask、不支持 dropout（`dropout_p` 必须 0）；
- head_dim 必须落在特定区间且（SM80 路径下）对齐 32；
- 为保持 torch.compile/autograd 兼容，前后向被「对称强制」绑定（见 u3-l2）。

它内部又分**两条 kernel 路径**：

- **SM90 专用路径**（`_ffpa_attn_forward_sm90` / `_ffpa_attn_backward_sm90`）：针对 Hopper、对称 `D≤512` 做了 head_dim 级特化（d384/d512/generic），加速比最高；
- **SM80 通用 Split-D 路径**（`_ffpa_attn_forward_sm80` / `_ffpa_attn_backward_sm80`）：用 Ampere 级 warp MMA + cp.async 写的通用 Split-D，向前兼容到 SM89/SM90/SM120，覆盖**所有其它支持的架构**以及 **SM90 上 `D>512`** 的情况，是兜底路径。

本讲的全部 4 个最小模块，本质上都在回答同一个问题：**给一个调用，该不该用 CuTeDSL？该用哪条路径？**

#### 4.1.2 核心流程：一次调用如何从分发层走到 CuTeDSL

承接 u3-4 的 `_FFPAAttnFunc.forward`，dense 路径的接驳如下（varlen 路径见 u2-l5、u6-l2）：

```
ffpa_attn_func(..., backend="cutedsl")
  └─ FFPAAttnMeta.fallback()          # hardware/head_dim 门禁：用 cute_forward_available / cute_max_supported_head_dim
       └─ normalize_inputs()          # attn_mask / dropout 在此直接抛错（无静默回退）

CuTeDSL 是 FFPA 四后端里**性能上限最高、约束也最多**的一个。它用 NVIDIA CUTLASS 的 CuTe DSL 在运行期 JIT 出 GPU kernel，能直接用上 Hopper 的 WGMMA + TMA，因此在 H200 SXM、`D=512` 大 head_dim 场景下可达 **427 TFLOPS**，是当前最快路径（[README.md:87](https://github.com/xlite-dev/ffpa-attn/blob/882989c2a3aa465867de9ea2dbd19dbed08660d9/README.md#L87)）。代价是：

- 只支持 fp16/bf16；
- 不支持 attn_mask、不支持 dropout（`dropout_p` 必须 0）；
- head_dim 必须落在特定区间且（SM80 路径下）对齐 32；
- 为保持 torch.compile/autograd 兼容，前后向被「对称强制」绑定（见 u3-l2）。

它内部又分**两条 kernel 路径**：

- **SM90 专用路径**（`_ffpa_attn_forward_sm90` / `_ffpa_attn_backward_sm90`）：针对 Hopper、对称 `D≤512` 做了 head_dim 级特化（d384/d512/generic），加速比最高；
- **SM80 通用 Split-D 路径**（`_ffpa_attn_forward_sm80` / `_ffpa_attn_backward_sm80`）：用 Ampere 级 warp MMA + cp.async 写的通用 Split-D，向前兼容到 SM89/SM90/SM120，覆盖**所有其它支持的架构**以及 **SM90 上 `D>512`** 的情况，是兜底路径。

本讲的全部 4 个最小模块，本质上都在回答同一个问题：**给一个调用，该不该用 CuTeDSL？该用哪条路径？**

#### 4.1.2 核心流程：一次调用如何从分发层走到 CuTeDSL

承接 u3-4 的 `_FFPAAttnFunc.forward`，dense 路径的接驳如下（varlen 路径见 u2-l5、u6-l2）：

```
ffpa_attn_func(..., backend="cutedsl")
  └─ FFPAAttnMeta.fallback()          # hardware/head_dim 门禁：用 cute_forward_available / cute_max_supported_head_dim
       └─ normalize_inputs()          # attn_mask / dropout 在此直接抛错（无静默回退）
